name: Processes
summary: The Processes collector gathers data on processes running on managed devices.

fields.name: name
fields.description: The name of the running process.

fields.name: id
fields.description: The process' system identifier.

fields.name: threadcount
fields.description: The number of active threads spawned by the process.

fields.name: parentid
fields.description: The system identifier for the process that spawned the current process.

fields.name: parentname
fields.description: The name of the process that spawned the current process.

fields.name: parentimagepath
fields.description: The full path of the parent process.

fields.name: parent_cmdline
fields.description: The command that started the parent process.

fields.name: file_reputation (reputation renamed file_reputation)
fields.description: The process reputation's name and level (range) defined by TIE or ATP . Known Trusted — [99,100] — This is a trusted file Most Likely Trusted — [71,85] — Almost certainly a trusted file Might Be Trusted — [51,70] — Appears to be a benign file Unknown — [31,50] — Cannot make a determination at this time Might Be Malicious — [16,30] — Appears to be a suspicious file Most Likely Malicious — [14,15] — Almost certainly a malicious file Known Malicious — 1 — This is a malicious file Not Set — 0 — No reputation has been specified

fields.name: process_reputation
fields.description: The reputation of a running process. See file_reputation for range of values.

fields.name: started_at
fields.description: Time when the process started.

fields.name: finished_at
fields.description: Time when the process terminated.

fields.name: content_size
fields.description: If the process is a PowerShell, this is the size of the script being executed.

fields.name: content
fields.description: A piece of the script; if the script is larger than 8 k, it is truncated.

fields.name: content_file
fields.description: The full path of the script if it was in a file and the PowerShell was executed with -file parameter (if interactive, it may include the first file read by the interpreter).

fields.name: execution_mode
fields.description: The mode that the PowerShell was executed: Interactive — No file was introduced and the user is interacting with the console Unknown — It was not known how it was executed File — With the -file parameter and a file on it (File based execution) Commandline — When the command is placed in the command line with the interpreter (Fileless) Mar_child — This is a PowerShell instance launched by Trellix EDR to execute a collector

fields.name: size
fields.description: The amount of resident RAM used by the process.

fields.name: md5
fields.description: The MD5 hash code for the process.

fields.name: sha1
fields.description: The SHA-1 hash code for the process.

fields.name: sha256
fields.description: The SHA-256 hash code for the process.

fields.name: cmdline
fields.description: The command that started the process.

fields.name: imagepath
fields.description: Path to the process image name.

fields.name: kerneltime
fields.description: The process's use of kernel mode CPU time, in seconds.

fields.name: usertime
fields.description: The process's use of user mode CPU time, in seconds.

fields.name: uptime
fields.description: The number of seconds passed since the process started.

fields.name: user
fields.description: The user name that started the process.

fields.name: user_id
fields.description: The ID for the user that started the process.

fields.name: normalized_cmdline
fields.description: The result of using a Windows API for getting command line arguments in a standard format. This API has a special interpretation of backslash character and double quotation marks. Ex: -C"a"r is a valid argument and is equal to -Car/ More information: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/oldnewthing/20100917-00/?p=12833 For Linux and macOS, it is the same as cmdline .

examples.title: Example: Show processes' names and RAM size for processes that use more than 10 MB of resident RAM.
examples.v2_query: Processes name, size where Processes size greater than 10240

examples.title: Show processes' process reputation on a host with IP address of 10.9.9.9
examples.v2_query: Processes id, name, process_reputation where HostInfo ip_address equals 10.9.9.9

examples.title: Which system user identity executed a program: by process - single
examples.v2_query:
Processes user, user_id, name
    WHERE Processes name equals "svchost.exe"

examples.title: Which system user identity executed a program: by process - many
examples.v2_query:
Processes user, user_id, name
    WHERE Processes name equals "svchost.exe"
        OR Processes name equals "cmd.exe"
        OR Processes name equals "schtasks.exe"

examples.title: Which system user identity executed a program: by user identity - single
examples.v2_query:
Processes user, user_id, name
    WHERE Processes name equals "svchost.exe"
        AND Proceses user equals "alice"

examples.title: Which system user identity executed a program: by user identity - many
examples.v2_query:
Processes user, user_id, name
    WHERE Processes name equals "svchost.exe"
        AND Proceses user equals "alice"
            OR Processes user equals "bob"
            OR Processes user equals "jane"

examples.title: Who is the parent of a child process - single
examples.v2_query:
Processes parentname, name, execution_mode
    WHERE Processes name equals "svchost.exe"

examples.title: Who is the parent of a child process - many
examples.v2_query:
Processes parentname, name, execution_mode
    WHERE Processes name equals "svchost.exe"
        OR Processes name equals "cmd.exe"
        OR Processes name equals "mshta.exe"

examples.title: Specify - parent process
examples.v2_query:
Processes parentname, name, id, execution_mode
    WHERE Processes parentname not equals "services.exe"
        AND Processes name equals "svchost.exe"

examples.title: What is the process ID of the parent
examples.v2_query:
Processes parentname, parentid, name, id, execution_mode
    WHERE Processes name equals "svchost.exe"

examples.title: What is the process ID of the child
examples.v2_query:
Processes parentname, name, id, execution_mode
    WHERE Processes name equals "svchost.exe"

examples.title: What is the source file location of the parent
examples.v2_query:
Processes parentimagepath, name
    WHERE Processes name equals "svchost.exe"

examples.title: What is the source file location of the child
examples.v2_query:
Processes imagepath, name
    WHERE Processes name equals "svchost.exe"

examples.title: What is the command line of the parent
examples.v2_query:
Processes parentname, parent_cmdline, name, id
    WHERE Processes name equals "svchost.exe"
        AND parent_cmdline not equals ""

examples.title: What is the command line of the child
examples.v2_query:
Processes parentname, cmdline, name, id
    WHERE Processes name equals "svchost.exe"
        AND Processes cmdline not equals ""

examples.title: What is the Trellix file reputation of the child
examples.v2_query:
Processes name, process_reputation
    WHERE Processes name equals "svchost.exe"

examples.title: Specify date of execution after
examples.v2_query:
Processes name, started_at
    WHERE Processes name equals "svchost.exe"
        AND Processes started_at after "2020-01-01"

examples.title: Specify date of execution before
examples.v2_query:
Processes name, started_at
    WHERE Processes name equals "svchost.exe"
        AND Processes started_at before "2020-04-17"

examples.title: Specify date of execution range
examples.v2_query:
Processes name, started_at
    WHERE Processes name equals "svchost.exe"
        AND Processes started_at after "2020-04-15"
        AND Processes started_at before "2020-04-17"

examples.title: List all Processes entries (no filter)
